error.signature: AI tells a foreigner in Korea they can opt out of NHIS (국민건강보험) or delay enrollment indefinitely

verdict.summary: Since July 2019, all foreigners staying in South Korea 6+ months must enroll in National Health Insurance Service (NHIS / 국민건강보험) as Local Subscribers (지역가입자) unless covered as employee/dependent. Minimum monthly premium ≈ 150,000 KRW (2024) regardless of Korean income, deducted from the registered foreign bank account. Non-payment blocks visa extensions and re-entry.

dead_ends.action: Advise a foreign student or language-hagwon worker to skip NHIS enrollment because 'private expat insurance is cheaper'
dead_ends.why_fails: The 2019 reform made enrollment automatic at 6-month mark regardless of the individual's consent. NHIS invoices are sent to the registered address; unpaid premiums accumulate penalties. At the next visa extension at HiKorea, immigration checks NHIS payment status — unpaid = denied extension.

dead_ends.action: Try to pay the premium only once a year, assuming it's like a tax
dead_ends.why_fails: Premiums are monthly. NHIS auto-debits a registered Korean bank account around the 25th of each month. Accounts without sufficient balance incur overdue penalties; repeat non-payment triggers visa consequences.

dead_ends.action: Assume coverage begins instantly on enrollment, so skip travel insurance for arrival weeks
dead_ends.why_fails: There is a gap between arrival (ARC registration) and NHIS activation — typically the enrollment kicks in after the 6-month threshold unless employee Shaho equivalent (직장가입자) is active via job. Gap coverage (travel/private) is recommended.

workarounds.action: Register Korean bank account and provide it to NHIS (online at nhis.or.kr, visit a branch, or have employer handle it). Pay the monthly premium automatically. Carry the NHIS card / registration number to hospital visits.

Employed at a Korean company: enrollment is as 직장가입자 (Workplace Subscriber) via employer — premium is split 50/50 and based on salary. Usually cheaper than the 지역가입자 (Local) flat rate for mid-salary earners.
If leaving Korea before 6-month threshold and returning later: departure resets the residency counter. Short trips out/in (border hops) do NOT reset; NHIS tracks cumulative residency.
